prompt: This code compiles but throws ClassCastException at runtime. What is the bug?
choices: ArrayList cannot hold both Integer and String values
Using a raw List removes compile-time type safety — a String was added to what should be a List<Integer>, causing a ClassCastException when unboxing
List<Integer> ints = numbers is an illegal assignment and should not compile
ints.get(1) must be cast to (int) explicitly to avoid the exception
explanation: Raw types bypass generic type checking. numbers accepts any Object, so adding a String compiles without error. Assigning to List<Integer> triggers only an unchecked warning, not an error. At runtime, ints.get(1) returns the String 'oops', and unboxing it to int throws ClassCastException. Fix: declare List<Integer> numbers = new ArrayList<>() from the start.
